scenes.text: Introduction to Docker: Docker is a platform used to develop, ship, and run applications on containers. Containers are small, lightweight, and portable units in which you can develop and deploy applications.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A(Docker) --> B(Containers) --> C(Applications); A --> D(Develop, Ship, Run);

scenes.text: Docker Architecture: Docker utilizes a client-server architecture. The Docker client communicates with the Docker daemon, which does the heavy lifting of building, running, and distributing your Docker containers.
scenes.image_prompt: graph LR; E(Docker Client) -- Request --> F(Docker Daemon); F -- Operates --> G(Containers);

scenes.text: Docker Workflow: A typical Docker workflow begins with creating a Dockerfile to define container specifications. Next, you build the Docker image, and finally, run it as a container. This workflow enables consistent environments across development and production.
scenes.image_prompt: sequenceDiagram; participant Dev as Developer; participant Df as Dockerfile; participant Di as Docker Image; participant C as Container; Dev->>Df: Create; Dev->>Di: Build; Dev->>C: Run;
